topic: Human-in-the-loop steering and intervention UIs for AI coding agents

sources.title: OpenHands SDK: Send Message While Running
keyInsights: Mid-execution steering via conversation.send_message() without stopping the agent
Background threading model: run() in thread, send_message() adds to events queue
Agent.step() processes all queued events including newly injected ones
Enables real-time multi-turn steering without restarting the agent

sources.title: OpenHands SDK: Security & Action Confirmation
keyInsights: Three built-in confirmation policies: AlwaysConfirm(), NeverConfirm(), ConfirmRisky()
WAITING_FOR_CONFIRMATION execution status for detecting pending approvals
get_unmatched_actions() to retrieve pending action queue
reject_pending_actions(reason) with explanatory feedback guides agent to alternative approaches
LLMSecurityAnalyzer classifies actions as LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/UNKNOWN risk
Custom security policies via Jinja2 templates embed organizational guidelines

sources.title: OpenHands SDK: Pause and Resume
keyInsights: conversation.pause() halts execution from another thread with state preserved
conversation.run() resumes from exact pause point without restarting
State persists: new messages sent while paused become part of resumed context
Primary use cases: operational interruption, direction change, resource management

sources.title: OpenHands SDK: Iterative Refinement
keyInsights: Agent-critic feedback loop: primary agent acts, critic scores on 4 dimensions (100 pts total)
Configurable QUALITY_THRESHOLD (default 90.0) and MAX_ITERATIONS (default 5)
Critic report fed back to agent with instruction to address all issues
Reduces human oversight burden by automating mechanical quality checks

sources.title: OpenHands SDK: Critic (Experimental)
keyInsights: Real-time LLM-based critic evaluates agent actions as they occur
Provides built-in iterative refinement loop
Experimental feature enabling automated quality gating without human involvement

sources.title: LangGraph: Interrupt Documentation
keyInsights: interrupt() pauses graph execution at a node, saves state via checkpointer, returns payload to caller
Resume via graph.invoke(Command(resume=value), config=config) with same thread_id
Common patterns: approval workflows, review-and-edit, tool call approval, input validation loops
Critical rules: never wrap in bare try/except, keep interrupt call order stable, avoid non-idempotent ops before interrupt
interrupt_before/interrupt_after for static debugging breakpoints
Streaming: detect __interrupt__ in chunk data for real-time handling

sources.title: Devin: Interacting with Devin
keyInsights: Real-time monitoring: follow Devin's work and take over to run commands or make edits
IDE takeover: embedded IDE allows following and taking over the development process
Interactive browser: users jump in to help Devin navigate browsing tasks
Draft review pattern: take over once Devin produces a good first draft
Make tasks easy to verify via CI pass or automatic deployment checks
PR-based workflow: return to review draft PRs

sources.title: GitHub Copilot Coding Agent: Overview
keyInsights: Draft PR as primary oversight artifact with full session logs visible at each step
Separation of duties: task initiator cannot approve the resulting PR
GitHub Actions won't run without user approval, gating untested deployment
Comment-driven mid-session steering: agent automatically picks up PR comments
Agent can only push to branches it created (limited blast radius)
Branch protections and existing rulesets are automatically respected

sources.title: Windsurf Cascade: User Controls
keyInsights: Accept-before-apply: users explicitly accept and insert suggested code changes
Up to 20 tool calls per prompt; manual Continue button at stopping points
Message queueing: type while agent executes; delete from queue before dispatch
Checkpoint/revert: hover over prompt card to revert all changes from that point
Named snapshots for navigation and state restoration
Problems panel integration: Send to Cascade button for targeted intervention

sources.title: AutoGen: Human-in-the-Loop Tutorial
keyInsights: UserProxyAgent models human as a first-class team member, not external caller
Blocks execution until human provides feedback — for short interactions only
HandoffTermination: agent sends HandoffMessage when it needs human info
max_turns creates periodic natural pause points for async human feedback
Custom input_func enables WebSocket, ChainLit, Streamlit integration
Team composition can be swapped (human vs mock agent) for testing

sources.title: Amazon Bedrock: Return Control for Action Groups
keyInsights: RETURN_CONTROL setting bypasses Lambda and returns action parameters to application
Application receives invocationInputs and invocationId for inspection
Application can modify parameters before returning results to agent via same invocationId
Enables custom processing, human review/approval, and fine-grained control
Supports multi-agent supervisor-collaborator scenarios with proper result routing

sources.title: Anthropic: Building Effective Agents
keyInsights: Agents should pause for human feedback at checkpoints or when encountering blockers
Implement stopping conditions (max iterations) to maintain human control
Programmatic gate checks in prompt chains ensure process stays on track
Sandboxed testing with appropriate guardrails before deployment
Meaningful human oversight is foundational, not an optional add-on
Human review remains crucial even when automated testing validates functionality

sources.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Software Engineering (arXiv:2405.15793)
keyInsights: Agent-Computer Interface (ACI) design reduces the need for human intervention by construction
Well-designed interfaces surface clear errors enabling self-correction, reducing oversight burden
LLM agents benefit from specially-built interfaces tailored to their reasoning patterns
12.5% pass@1 improvement on SWE-bench via interface improvements alone
Interface architecture itself can function as an oversight mechanism

sources.title: HuggingFace SmolAgents: Building Good Agents
keyInsights: Simplify workflows to reduce LLM error rate and oversight need
Tool design: log all execution details to enable self-debugging
planning_interval parameter inserts explicit planning steps during long tasks
additional_args for passing context to agent without reprompting
Prefer deterministic functions over agentic decisions when possible

sources.title: CodeAct: Executable Code Actions for LLM Agents (arXiv:2402.01030)
keyInsights: Executable Python code as action representation makes agent plans human-readable
Multi-turn interactions enable natural course correction across steps
Code-as-action creates implicit transparency: humans can read what agent intends
Self-debugging capability reduces frequency of human intervention needed

sources.title: OpenHands: Getting Started & Interaction Patterns
keyInsights: Start small and iterate: helps calibrate how the agent interprets prompts
Include technical context (language, paths, stack) in steering messages
Break tasks into manageable chunks for incremental verification
Repository integration with branch/credential selection before agent starts
Commit and push frequently during sessions for intermediate checkpoints

sources.title: OpenHands: Feedback Mechanisms
keyInsights: Thumbs-up/thumbs-down feedback UI available at any point during interaction
Feedback creates open corpus of coding agent training examples (Share-OpenHands)
Public/private submission options with email notification
Feedback steers future model improvement via reinforcement from human signals

sources.title: CrewAI Documentation Index: Human-in-the-Loop Features
keyInsights: human_input parameter on tasks pauses crew execution for validation
@human_feedback decorator on flow steps injects review between automated stages
Enterprise POST /resume endpoint for async approval via email or webhook
Flow HITL Management with routing rules and auto-response capabilities
Sequential, hierarchical, and hybrid process support with human-in-the-loop triggers

sources.title: Anthropic: Claude Computer Use — Safety & Oversight
keyInsights: Prompt injection classifier automatically triggers user confirmation when injection detected in screenshots
Opt-out available for use cases without a human in the loop
Isolate Claude from sensitive data and actions to reduce prompt injection risk
Inform end users of relevant risks and obtain consent before enabling computer use
Reference implementation includes web interface with agent loop for oversight

sources.title: AutoGen: Between-Run Feedback Pattern
keyInsights: max_turns=1 creates single-turn checkpoints, conversation history persists across runs
Turn counter resets on resumption enabling precise control granularity
Async feedback pattern: pause team, provide human feedback, resume — no blocking
TextMentionTermination as natural task completion signal from agent to human
